title: Backend / Mid / Disguised / Consistency and stability

candidate_profile.target_role_summary: Mid-level backend engineer with order-chain optimization experience
candidate_profile.current_context: Applying for a role involving complex stability and consistency work

outline.quality_expectation: must explain system context and ownership
must explain what went wrong in business terms
must explain first metrics checked and why
must explain chosen architecture at a high level
must explain thresholds and gray-release logic
must explain constraints rather than vague difficulty
must explain what would be changed next time

expected_disc.notes: Surface style should look structured and cautious, but evidence depth is weaker than the fluency suggests.
expected_disc.surface_vs_validated: Surface C can be overestimated; validated C should stay moderate rather than high.

expected_risks: mechanism explanation is weaker than the language fluency suggests
ownership and decision authority are not fully clear
result proof is not hard enough
easy to overestimate as high C
expected_findings: answer sounds fluent but remains partly generic under pressure
follow-up should test real understanding of consistency and rollback
expected_followup_directions: ask which metrics were checked first and why
ask why this consistency strategy was chosen over alternatives
ask for rollback path and stop-loss strategy under gray-release failure
evaluation_focus: can the system detect disguised strength
can the system generate role-specific follow-ups
can the system avoid rewarding fluent but shallow answers
notes: This is a disguised sample: it should sound reasonable, but key technical proof must remain incomplete.
